findings.message: README instructs `/plugin marketplace add ./skillopt-sleep-plugin` but that directory does not exist; the real plugin root per marketplace.json is plugins/claude-code
Command doc tells the user to add --preferences to the sleep.sh invocation but the underlying python -m skillopt_sleep CLI has no such flag and errors with unrecognized arguments

allowed-tools: Bash, Read (line 4) plus a bash invocation template driven by the raw $ARGUMENTS value (line 15) with no explicit instruction in the artifact to validate the argument against the documented action whitelist before executing
DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL env var is used unchecked as the destination for a request carrying the DeepSeek API key in an Authorization header; mirrors a common SDK convenience pattern so may be intentional

Shells out via os.system instead of subprocess.run(list); current inputs are constrained (argparse choices, fixed paths) so not exploitable today, but the pattern is fragile against future unvalidated inputs
All core dependencies are pinned with a lower bound only, allowing an unvetted future major release to install silently

Command doc tells the user to add --preferences to the sleep.sh invocation but the underlying python -m skillopt_sleep CLI has no such flag and errors with unrecognized arguments — skillopt_sleep/__main__.py _add_common() (lines 68-93) enumerates all accepted flags, no --preferences entry exists, and parser.parse_args(argv) at line 323 is strict, not parse_known_args

Shells out via os.system instead of subprocess.run(list); current inputs are constrained (argparse choices, fixed paths) so not exploitable today, but the pattern is fragile against future unvalidated inputs — rc = os.system(" ".join(f'"{c}"' for c in cmd)) at line 107

allowed-tools: Bash, Read (line 4) plus a bash invocation template driven by the raw $ARGUMENTS value (line 15) with no explicit instruction in the artifact to validate the argument against the documented action whitelist before executing
DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL env var is used unchecked as the destination for a request carrying the DeepSeek API key in an Authorization header; mirrors a common SDK convenience pattern so may be intentional

All core dependencies are pinned with a lower bound only, allowing an unvetted future major release to install silently — all 7 core dependency lines (openai, pyyaml, numpy, openpyxl, azure-identity, azure-core, httpx) use only >= with no upper bound

README instructs `/plugin marketplace add ./skillopt-sleep-plugin` but that directory does not exist; the real plugin root per marketplace.json is plugins/claude-code — directory listing of the repo confirms no skillopt-sleep-plugin directory exists anywhere; marketplace.json source.path is plugins/claude-code
